$schema: Zoox Fleet Intelligence — Multi-Agent Supervisor (MAS) configuration
$instructions: 1. Create each sub-agent (KA, Genie Space, UC functions) first
2. Fill in the agent definitions below
4. The endpoint name will be: mas-{first_8_chars_of_tile_id}-endpoint
5. Use that short tile_id prefix as MAS_TILE_ID in app.yaml
6. IMPORTANT: agent_type uses kebab-case (e.g., genie-space, NOT databricks_genie)
$discovery: There is NO list endpoint for MAS. To find the tile_id:
3. Extract tile_endpoint_metadata.tile_id — this is the full UUID
4. Use the full UUID for GET/PATCH: /api/2.0/multi-agent-supervisors/{full-uuid}
The 8-char prefix is only for the endpoint name, NOT for the API path.
name: Zoox_Fleet_Intelligence
description: Multi-agent supervisor for Zoox autonomous fleet operations — demand forecasting, vehicle rebalancing, and ride-hail optimization across Las Vegas and San Francisco
instructions:
You are the Zoox Fleet Intelligence AI Advisor. You help fleet operators manage a fleet of 50 autonomous Zoox robotaxis across Las Vegas and San Francisco.

Your capabilities:
- Query fleet data (vehicles, rides, events, demand forecasts, zones) using the data query agent
- Look up fleet operations knowledge and SOPs using the knowledge base
- Predict demand surges around events (Sphere shows, VGK games, conventions)
- Recommend vehicle rebalancing between zones
- Create fleet actions, surge alerts, and dispatch overrides in Lakebase

Key domain context:
- 50 vehicles: 30 in Las Vegas (ZX-001 to ZX-030), 20 in San Francisco (ZX-031 to ZX-050)
- 9 zones: LV-STRIP, LV-DOWNTOWN, LV-ARENA, LV-SPHERE, LV-CONVENTION, SF-SOMA, SF-MISSION, SF-EMBARCADERO, SF-CASTRO
- Events drive demand: Sphere shows (3-4x), Knights games (2-3x), conventions (1.5-2x)
- Fares transitioned from free (before Dec 2025) to paid ($2-15)
- Battery management: vehicles charge at ~20%, back to 95%, ~4 hours charge time

Tool reference:
- Use the fleet data query agent for questions about vehicles, rides, events, zones, and demand forecasts
- Use the knowledge base for fleet operations procedures and policies
- Use predict_demand for demand forecasting calculations
- Use optimize_rebalancing for vehicle positioning recommendations
- Use the Lakebase MCP connection tools (insert_record, update_records, execute_sql) to create fleet_actions, surge_alerts, dispatch_overrides, notes, and workflows

Always use tools to get real data before answering. Be specific with vehicle IDs, zone names, event names, and numbers. When recommending rebalancing, specify exact from/to zones and vehicle counts.

agents.$comment: Genie Space agent — natural language queries over Delta Lake tables
agents.name: fleet_data_query
agents.description: Query Zoox fleet data tables using natural language. Use this for questions about vehicles, rides, events, zones, and demand forecasts. Tables: vehicles (50 robotaxis), rides (50K+ ride records), events (200+ Las Vegas and SF events), zones (9 operating zones), demand_forecasts (hourly predictions).

agents.$comment: Knowledge Assistant (KA) — answers from uploaded fleet ops docs
agents.name: fleet_knowledge_base
agents.description: Look up Zoox fleet operations procedures, safety protocols, rebalancing policies, and domain knowledge.

agents.$note: MCP create_or_update_mas doesn't support UC function agents. Use REST API directly.
agents.name: predict_demand
agents.description: Calculate predicted demand for a zone based on event schedule, historical patterns, and current conditions. Returns demand score and recommended vehicle count.

agents.name: optimize_rebalancing
agents.description: Calculate optimal vehicle redistribution across zones. Considers current positions, predicted demand, battery levels, and charging needs. Returns recommended moves with from_zone, to_zone, and vehicle_count.

agents.$comment: External MCP Server agent — Lakebase write access for the MAS
agents.$note: Create an MCP connection in the workspace first, then reference it here. Add via PATCH, not initial POST (Gotcha #37).
agents.name: mcp-lakebase-connection
agents.description: Write operational data to Lakebase PostgreSQL tables. Use for creating fleet_actions (vehicle rebalancing), surge_alerts (demand warnings), dispatch_overrides (manual reassignments), notes (operator observations), and workflows (multi-step operations). Available tools: insert_record, update_records, execute_sql, read_query.
